quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 7 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 7 Quiz 12: Logarithmic & Exponential Functions — Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 7: Logarithmic & Exponential Functions — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: Condense into a single logarithm: $\frac{1}{3}\log_2(x) - 2\log_2(y) + \log_2(z) + 1$.
questions.explanation: Convert coefficients to exponents: $\log_2(x^{1/3}) - \log_2(y^2) + \log_2(z)$. The constant $1 = \log_2(2)$. Combine: $\log_2(\frac{\sqrt[3]{x} \cdot z \cdot 2}{y^2}) = \log_2(\frac{2z\sqrt[3]{x}}{y^2})$.

questions.question: Earthquake A has magnitude 8.5. Earthquake B has magnitude 5.0. How many times more energy does A release? Use $E \propto 10^{1.5M}$.
questions.explanation: Energy ratio = $10^{1.5(8.5) - 1.5(5.0)} = 10^{1.5(3.5)} = 10^{5.25} \approx 178000$. Each magnitude increase of 1 multiplies energy by $10^{1.5} \approx 31.6$.

questions.question: $\$4000$ is invested: Account X at 6% compounded annually, Account Y at 5.8% compounded continuously. Which is larger after 12 years and by how much? Use $1.06^{12} \approx 2.0122$ and $e^{0.696} \approx 2.0057$.
questions.explanation: Account X: $4000(2.0122) = 8048.80$. Account Y: $4000(2.0057) = 8022.80$. Account X wins by $\$26$. The higher nominal rate outweighs the continuous compounding advantage.

questions.question: After 20 days, 25 grams of a radioactive substance remain. The half-life is 8 days. What was the initial mass?
A cup of coffee at 190°F is placed in a 70°F room with $k = 0.12$. How long until it cools to 100°F? Use $\ln 4 \approx 1.3863$.
A data set: $(0, 100)$, $(1, 130)$, $(2, 169)$, $(3, 219.7)$. Identify the model type and write the function.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 7: nested log evaluation, condensing with constant terms, exponential quadratics, log equations with fractions, Richter energy comparisons, annual vs. continuous growth, half-life with unknown initial mass, Newton's Law of Cooling, model identification from data, and systems with logs.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) $\log_b(b^k) = k$ — use this for nested logs, (2) The constant 1 condenses as $\log_b(b)$, (3) For $4^x - 6(2^x) + 8 = 0$, substitute $u = 2^x$, (4) Energy ratio for Richter: $10^{1.5(M_1 - M_2)}$.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Condensing: coefficients become exponents, addition becomes multiplication, subtraction becomes division, (2) For half-life with unknown initial mass, solve $M_0 = \frac{M}{(0.5)^{t/h}}$, (3) Newton's Law: isolate $e^{-kt}$, take $\ln$, (4) Systems with logs become linear systems after substitution.
